strategy: It wasn't really clear to me if the points for different mushrooms were consistent across the levels. It appears they were. I tried to focus on independent elements, like color, shape and stem width. Unfortunately, this wasn't particularly useful, as it appeared none of these were determinant. Instead, it was the combinations of the three that were important. The exception seemed to be that all red mushrooms had a positive value. All pink/fuchsia may have had negative value, but it was unclear. Because I wasn't able to figure out a way to isolate the individual elements, I found it rather difficult. With the exception of the red ones. On a few levels I tried to just keep going collecting reds. But, even when I played a single level for a long time it did not seem like it progressed it much further than levels where I hit \P\" as soon as I was permitted to move forward. I don't want to sound like I was complaining when I said it was unenjoyable
color_rank_most_to_least: but the difficulty for me definitely frustrated me. When I don't feel like I'm doing well at something
color_rank_array: it motivates me to try harder. I suppose
room_rank_easiest_to_hardest: if a monetary award was connected to how well I did
room_rank_array: I probably would've created an excel sheet or something and taken detailed notes instead of trying to go just off of memory. "
